label: Kernels & implicit feature maps

node.label: Kernels & implicit feature maps
node.definition: Similarity functions represent high-dimensional features without explicitly constructing them.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Kernels & implicit feature maps is modeled as depending on Linear algebra, Probability theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Support vector & kernel machines, Gaussian processes.
examples: atlas downstream link to Support vector & kernel machines
atlas downstream link to Gaussian processes
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Probability theory.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Kernels & implicit feature maps has a `uses` dependency on Linear algebra because Linear algebra is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Kernels & implicit feature maps has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Kernels & implicit feature maps has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
